title: A Systematic Survey of Semantic Role Labeling in the Era of Pretrained Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2502.08660v4 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Semantic role labeling (SRL) is a central natural language processing task for understanding predicate-argument structures within texts and enabling downstream applications. Despite extensive research, comprehensive surveys that critically synthesize the field from a unified perspective remain lacking. This survey makes several contributions beyond organizing existing work. We propose a unified four-dimensional taxonomy that categorizes SRL research along model architectures, syntax feature modeling, application scenarios, and multimodal extensions. We provide a critical analysis of when and why syntactic features help, identifying conditions under which syntax-aided approaches provide consistent gains over syntax-free counterparts. We offer the first systematic treatment of SRL in the era of large language models, examining the complementary roles of LLMs and specialized SRL systems and identifying directions for hybrid approaches.

Title: A Systematic Survey of Semantic Role Labeling in the Era of Pretrained Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2502.08660v4 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Semantic role labeling (SRL) is a central natural language processing task for understanding predicate-argument structures within texts and enabling downstream applications. Despite extensive research, comprehensive surveys that critically synthesize the field from a unified perspective remain lacking. This survey makes several contributions beyond organizing existing work. We propose a unified four-dimensional taxonomy that categorizes SRL research along model architectures, syntax feature modeling, application scenarios, and multimodal extensions. We provide a critical analysis of when and why syntactic features help, identifying conditions under which syntax-aided approaches provide consistent gains over syntax-free counterparts. We offer the first systematic treatment of SRL in the era of large language models, examining the complementary roles of LLMs and specialized SRL systems and identifying directions for hybrid approaches.
translated_title: 预训练语言模型时代语义角色标注的系统性综述
translated_abstract: arXiv:2502.08660v4 公告类型：替换 摘要：语义角色标注（SRL）是一项核心的自然语言处理任务，旨在理解文本中的谓词-论元结构，并支持下游应用。尽管已有大量研究，但缺乏从统一视角批判性综合该领域的全面综述。本文在整理现有工作之外做出了多项贡献。我们提出了一个统一的四维分类体系，从模型架构、句法特征建模、应用场景和多模态扩展四个维度对SRL研究进行分类。我们批判性地分析了句法特征何时及为何有用，识别了句法辅助方法相对于无句法方法能带来持续增益的条件。我们首次系统性地处理了大语言模型时代的SRL问题，探讨了LLMs与专用SRL系统的互补作用，并指出了混合方法的发展方向。
tldr: 本文提出了一个四维分类体系来系统性梳理预训练语言模型时代的语义角色标注研究，批判分析了句法特征的有效性条件，并首次探讨了大语言模型与专用SRL系统的互补关系。
en_tdlr: This survey proposes a unified four-dimensional taxonomy for Semantic Role Labeling in the era of pretrained language models, critically analyzes the conditions under which syntactic features help, and is the first to systematically examine the complementary roles of LLMs and specialized SRL systems.
